node.title: Uber - Coding round 1 - for SDE II

topic.title: Uber - Coding round 1 - for SDE II

post.content:
"You are given an array A of N positive integer values. A subarray of this array is called 
Odd-Even subarray if the number of odd integers in this subarray is equal to the number  
of even integers in this subarray.

Find the number of Odd-Even subarrays for the given array.

ex- arr[4]=[4,5,5,5]

ans= we have only [1,2] subarray which satisfy this so ans=1
contraints: N<= 10^5"

**Feel free to post the answer  or link to the answer**

**Try to find an O(N) solution**
Convert the array first where odd num = -1, eve num = 1, now problem is to count the number of subarrays with sum 0. Standard problem of subarrays sum equals k using hashmap O(n).

U can use hashing .Let c1 and c2 be the number of odd numbers and even numbers respectively.Number of subarrays ending at i having odd elements =even elements eqauls m1[c1-c2].After that Just increase freq of c1-c2 in the hashmap.

You can use hashing to find the number of subarrays using prefix sum. \nHash initially has 0->1, keep adding 1 for odd and -1 for even. At any given index hash[prefix_sum] is to be added to number of subarrays.
